pitch_rationale: The image shows a row of identical hip-roofed residential/townhouse buildings in Thornton, CO (Denver suburb). Several visual cues were assessed: (1) Shadow length — the north-side ground shadows cast by the eaves are pronounced but not extreme; using the 50-ft scale bar (≈266 px), the building footprints measure roughly 35–45 ft in the N–S direction, and shadows extend approximately 10–18 ft beyond the eave line, consistent with a moderate solar altitude and a moderate roof pitch in the 5:12–6:12 range. (2) Roof-plane visibility — from directly overhead, the four hip planes are clearly visible and cover a moderate portion of the building footprint; very steep roofs (≥8:12) would show markedly wider, more dominant planes, while shallow roofs (≤4:12) would show very little plane width — these fall in between, pointing to ~5:12. (3) Ridge-to-eave geometry — the hip ridges converge toward a relatively short central point/ridge, and the triangular hip ends are moderately prominent, consistent with a 5:12 pitch. (4) Regional context — Thornton, CO multifamily/townhouse construction commonly uses 5:12–6:12 for adequate snow-shed without extreme structural cost. Taken together, 5:12 is the best single estimate, with 6:12 as a close alternative.
footprint_rationale: Targeted the right-center building in the middle row of three identical multi-unit townhouse clusters, which aligns with the E 102nd Ct cul-de-sac visible in the lower right. Bounding box traced at approximately x:710–950, y:445–665 (240×220 px). Using the stated scale of 1 px = 0.1879 ft, this yields roughly 43.2 ft E–W × 41.3 ft N–S = 1,785 sq ft gross. Minor front-face projections and step-backs are folded into the net estimate of 1,820 sq ft. The roof is a pure hip style (no gable/rake edges visible); the distinctive '4'-shadow on each building is cast by the hip ridge lines onto the lower roof planes. One main ridge (~135 px) runs E–W at the peak; four hip lines (~110 px each) descend diagonally to the building corners; two short valley lines (~50 px each) appear at the south-side bump-out junctions. Eave runs the full perimeter of all four sides. Confidence is moderate-low due to tree canopy partially obscuring corners, uncertainty in exact unit vs. building address boundary, and potential pixel-boundary estimation error of ±10%.
